    st.append(i)

prompt: Add `enqueue(self, x)` to `Queue`, which writes `x` at index `(self.head + self.size) % self.n` and increments `self.size`.
tests:
q = Queue(3)
q.enqueue(10); q.enqueue(20)
assert q.size == 2, q.size
assert q.a[0] == 10 and q.a[1] == 20

prompt: Add `dequeue(self)` to `Queue`. Read the value at `self.head`, advance `self.head` to `(self.head + 1) % self.n`, decrement `self.size`, and return the value.
solution:
class Queue:
    def __init__(self, capacity):
        self.a = [None] * capacity
        self.n = capacity
        self.head = 0
        self.size = 0

    def enqueue(self, x):
        self.a[(self.head + self.size) % self.n] = x
        self.size += 1

    def dequeue(self):
        v = self.a[self.head]
        self.head = (self.head + 1) % self.n
        self.size -= 1
        return v
tests:
q = Queue(3)
q.enqueue(10); q.enqueue(20); q.enqueue(30)
assert q.dequeue() == 10
assert q.head == 1 and q.size == 2
q.enqueue(40)
assert q.a[0] == 40
assert q.dequeue() == 20

solution:
class Deque:
    def __init__(self, capacity):
        self.a = [None] * capacity
        self.n = capacity
        self.head = 0
        self.size = 0

    def append(self, x):
        self.a[(self.head + self.size) % self.n] = x
        self.size += 1

    def popleft(self):
        v = self.a[self.head]
        self.head = (self.head + 1) % self.n
        self.size -= 1
        return v

    def appendleft(self, x):
        self.head = (self.head - 1 + self.n) % self.n
        self.a[self.head] = x
        self.size += 1
tests:
d = Deque(4)
d.append(2); d.append(3)
d.appendleft(1); d.appendleft(0)
assert d.size == 4
assert d.popleft() == 0
assert d.popleft() == 1
assert d.popleft() == 2

solution:
class Deque:
    def __init__(self, capacity):
        self.a = [None] * capacity
        self.n = capacity
        self.head = 0
        self.size = 0

    def append(self, x):
        self.a[(self.head + self.size) % self.n] = x
        self.size += 1

    def popleft(self):
        v = self.a[self.head]
        self.head = (self.head + 1) % self.n
        self.size -= 1
        return v

    def appendleft(self, x):
        self.head = (self.head - 1 + self.n) % self.n
        self.a[self.head] = x
        self.size += 1

    def pop(self):
        self.size -= 1
        return self.a[(self.head + self.size) % self.n]
tests:
d = Deque(4)
d.append(1); d.append(2); d.append(3)
assert d.pop() == 3
assert d.pop() == 2
d.appendleft(0)
assert d.pop() == 1
assert d.popleft() == 0

prompt: What does this evaluate to?
snippet:
from collections import deque
nums = [1, 3, -1]
d = deque()
for i, x in enumerate(nums):
    while d and nums[d[-1]] <= x:
        d.pop()
    d.append(i)
list(d)
